title: Suspected leak — stop DIY
summary: If moisture returns, a wall is wet, or there is a musty smell, do not treat it as a normal grout issue.
steps: Dry the place and check whether moisture returns.
Do not cover the problem with new sealant.
Contact a professional or building administration.
warnings: New sealant does not repair a leak or damaged installation.
dontDo: do not seal an active leak
do not ignore wall moisture

title: Suspected leak — safety checklist
summary: Minimum plan to secure the place and collect information before calling help.
steps: Dry the place and take photos now and after 15–30 minutes.
Check whether moisture appears after using shower, sink or basin.
Limit using this place until checked if possible.
Do not remove tiles or large parts without diagnosis.
Write down symptoms: when water appears, smell, color change.
Call a professional if the problem returns or affects wall/floor.
warnings: Moisture behind surfaces may need diagnosis, not cosmetic repair.
dontDo: do not cover wet area with sealant
do not use strong chemicals on unknown material

title: High risk — call a professional
summary: At high risk the app does not guide through DIY repair.
steps: Stop work.
Dry visible water only if safe.
Consult a professional.
warnings: High risk may mean installation, substrate or hidden moisture.
dontDo: do not dismantle surfaces without knowledge
do not hide the problem under grout

title: High risk — checklist before help
summary: Collect facts and limit damage instead of doing a cosmetic fix.
steps: Take photos of the place, crack, detachment or moisture.
Write when the issue appears and whether it grows.
Limit water use in this area if it is a bathroom.
Do not apply a new layer over wet or unstable material.
Prepare description for a professional: place, material, duration, symptoms.
After diagnosis decide if sealant/grout replacement is enough.
warnings: Cosmetic repair can delay finding a serious cause.
dontDo: do not seal wet surfaces
do not use strong chemicals without material check

title: Dirty grout — basic cleaning
summary: For regular dirt start with gentle cleaning and a small hidden test.
steps: Vacuum or sweep the grout.
Wash with warm water and mild cleaner.
Scrub with a soft brush and rinse.
warnings: Natural stone needs caution with acids and strong cleaners.
dontDo: do not start with harsh chemicals
do not use a metal brush

title: Dirty grout — full guide
summary: Cleaning grout without damaging nearby surfaces.
steps: Identify material near grout: ceramic, acrylic, stone or unknown.
Test cleaner in a hidden area.
Apply mild cleaner and wait a few minutes.
Brush along grout line without scratching tile.
Rinse with clean water and dry well.
If grout is crumbling, deeply stained or soft, plan repair instead of more scrubbing.
warnings: On natural stone avoid vinegar and acidic products unless confirmed safe.
dontDo: do not mix chemical products
do not scrape tile with sharp metal

title: Old sealant — likely replacement
summary: Old, hard, cracked or stained sealant is usually better removed and replaced.
steps: Remove loose and detached fragments.
Clean and dry the area.
Do not apply new sealant over the old layer.
warnings: Sealant needs a dry and clean surface.
dontDo: do not apply new sealant over dirty old sealant
do not work on a wet surface

title: Old sealant — full replacement
summary: Steps for simple DIY sealant replacement.
steps: Cut old sealant carefully, keeping blade away from tub, tray or counter surface.
Remove residue with plastic scraper or suitable tool.
Clean, degrease and dry the place very well.
Tape edges with painter tape for a straight line.
Apply sanitary sealant in one continuous pass and smooth it.
Remove tape before curing starts and let it cure according to product instructions.
warnings: If it is wet or soft under old sealant, stop and check the cause.
dontDo: do not use shower too soon
do not apply thick layer over dirt

title: Mold on sealant — check depth
summary: Surface film may be cleaned, but mold inside sealant means replacement.
steps: Wear gloves and ventilate the room.
Clean the surface with bathroom-safe cleaner.
If stains return or are inside the sealant, plan replacement.
warnings: Mold may indicate ventilation or constant moisture issue.
dontDo: do not mix chemicals
do not ignore poor ventilation

title: Mold on sealant — procedure
summary: Decide between cleaning, replacement and preventing return.
steps: Check whether stain is surface-only or deep in sealant.
Ventilate and wear gloves.
Clean with a product safe for nearby material.
Dry and observe whether the stain returns after days.
If sealant is black inside, cracked or detached, remove and replace with sanitary sealant.
Improve habits: wipe after showering, ventilate, do not cover vents.
warnings: Returning mold is often a moisture problem, not only dirty surface.
dontDo: do not combine chemicals
do not close a wet bathroom without ventilation

title: Crack — check stability
summary: A small crack may be repaired, but moving substrate or a wide gap needs diagnosis.
steps: Check whether the crack grows.
Clean and dry the place.
Stop DIY if there is movement or water.
warnings: A crack may mean movement or substrate problem.
dontDo: do not fill an active crack without diagnosis

title: Crack — full assessment
summary: Determine whether cosmetic repair is enough or help is needed.
steps: Take a close and wider photo of the crack.
Check whether the element moves under gentle pressure.
Clean and dry the place without forcing the gap wider.
If crack is small and dry, consider material-compatible repair.
If it returns, grows or moisture appears, consult a professional.
Observe the place for a few days after repair.
warnings: Repair filler will not solve moving substrate.
dontDo: do not cover moisture
do not apply material over dirt and water

title: Detached material — do not cover over
summary: Loose sealant or grout usually needs removal of detached part and clean base.
steps: Remove only loose fragments.
Check for moisture underneath.
If dry, prepare for replacement.
warnings: Covering over detachment usually fails quickly.
dontDo: do not seal detachment without cleaning

title: Detached material — full guide
summary: Remove loose material and prepare for a new layer.
steps: Find the full length of detachment, not only visible part.
Remove loose material carefully without damaging nearby surface.
Clean the gap from residue, dust and grease.
Check that the place is dry and stable.
Prepare painter tape and apply material matched to the use.
If detachment returns or base is soft, diagnose the cause.
warnings: Detachment in wet zones may hide water behind the surface.
dontDo: do not leave dust under new layer
do not work on wet base

title: Basic sealant or grout assessment
summary: First decide whether the issue is surface-level or needs replacement.
steps: Identify dirt, crack, mold, detachment or moisture.
Clean a small test area.
Stop if water or soft base appears.
warnings: Not every joint is suitable for quick repair.
dontDo: do not cover the problem without checking

title: Universal sealant and grout procedure
summary: Choose between cleaning, refresh, replacement and professional help.
steps: Check the issue: dirt, age, mold, crack, detachment or leak.
Identify nearby material: ceramic, acrylic, stone or unknown.
For regular dirt start with mild cleaning.
For old, cracked or detached sealant plan replacement.
For moisture, leak, soft base or installation problem stop DIY.
After work dry the area and observe whether the problem returns.
warnings: The app helps with simple work but does not diagnose installation or hidden moisture.
dontDo: do not mix chemicals
do not apply material on wet base
